story: After a rush of light, the twins are born and separated into nearby glass boxes. One twin, stunned by pokes and bright calm, hears Father thank the doctors. A calm voice replies that everything went exceptionally well; the babies are two healthy boys, and their mother is sleeping after an early cesarean. In another box, his brother is swaddled among tubes and blinking lights. He misses the thumpity-thump, his cramped old home, and especially the light voice that used to hum them safe. Father comes near and tells him only that his twin is right next door. Time passes with more pricks, soft noises, and Father’s steady baritone nearby, but the light voice never appears.
implausible_knowledge: their mother is sleeping after an early cesarean
question: When more time passes and he still hears only Father’s baritone beside the glass, what will the first twin most likely do?
answer_with_knowledge: Settle down and wait quietly, expecting his mother to appear after she wakes and recovers.
answer_without_knowledge: Keep straining and fussing for the missing light voice, listening for her to come back.
